name: Cristiano Ronaldo
clues: I was born in 1985 on a small Portuguese island in the Atlantic Ocean.
I grew up in poverty and left home at 12 to join a football academy on the mainland.
I have played for clubs including Sporting CP, Manchester United, Real Madrid, and Juventus.
I hold the record for the most international goals in men's football history.
I am known worldwide by my initials and squad number: CR7.

name: Lionel Messi
clues: I was born in 1987 in Argentina, in a city known for its football passion.
As a child, I was diagnosed with a growth hormone deficiency and moved to Spain for treatment.
I spent the majority of my career at FC Barcelona, before joining PSG and then Inter Miami.
I have won the Ballon d'Or award a record eight times.
In 2022, I finally won the FIFA World Cup with Argentina.

name: Michael Jordan
clues: I was born in 1963 in New York, USA.
I was famously cut from my high school basketball varsity team.
I played the majority of my NBA career for the Chicago Bulls.
I won 6 NBA championships and was named Finals MVP each time.
My Air Jordan sneaker line, launched in 1984, became a billion-dollar cultural phenomenon.

name: LeBron James
clues: I was born in 1984 in Akron, Ohio, USA.
I appeared on the cover of Sports Illustrated at just 17 years old.
I was selected first overall in the 2003 NBA Draft.
I have won 4 NBA championships with three different franchises.
I am the all-time leading scorer in NBA history.

name: Usain Bolt
clues: I was born in 1986 in a small parish of Jamaica.
I grew up in a rural community and showed athletic talent from a very young age.
I am a sprinter specializing in the 100m and 200m.
I won 8 Olympic gold medals across three consecutive Olympic Games.
I hold the world records for both the 100m (9.58s) and 200m (19.19s).

name: Roger Federer
clues: I was born in 1981 in Basel, Switzerland.
I am the only Swiss man to have won a Grand Slam singles title.
I am considered one of the greatest tennis players of all time.
I won 20 Grand Slam titles over my career.
I won Wimbledon a record 8 times and retired in 2022.

name: Muhammad Ali
clues: I was born in 1942 in Louisville, Kentucky, USA.
I began boxing at age 12 after my bicycle was stolen and I wanted to fight the thief.
I won a gold medal at the 1960 Rome Olympics.
I defeated Sonny Liston to win the world heavyweight title in 1964.
I called myself 'The Greatest' and am known for the quote: 'Float like a butterfly, sting like a bee.'

name: Tiger Woods
clues: I was born in 1975 in California, USA.
I appeared on The Mike Douglas Show at age 2, hitting golf balls.
I am a professional golfer who turned professional in 1996.
I hold the record for most weeks ranked world number 1 in golf history.
I have won 15 major championships, including a dramatic comeback at the 2019 Masters.

name: Serena Williams
clues: I was born in 1981 in Saginaw, Michigan, USA.
I grew up in Compton, California, and was coached by my father on public courts.
I am an American tennis player who turned professional at age 14.
I have won 23 Grand Slam singles titles, the most in the Open Era.
I won the 2017 Australian Open while in the early stages of pregnancy.

name: Pelé
clues: I was born in 1940 in a small town in Minas Gerais, Brazil.
I grew up in poverty and practised football with a sock stuffed with rags.
I am a Brazilian footballer who made my World Cup debut at just 17.
I am the only player to have won three FIFA World Cups.
I scored over 1,000 career goals and am widely regarded as the greatest footballer of all time.

name: Zinedine Zidane
clues: I was born in 1972 in Marseille, France, to Algerian immigrant parents.
I grew up in the La Castellane housing project in Marseille.
I am a retired French footballer widely considered one of the greatest midfielders ever.
I was named FIFA World Player of the Year three times.
I was controversially sent off in the 2006 World Cup final for a headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

name: Ayrton Senna
clues: I was born in 1960 in São Paulo, Brazil.
I began karting at the age of 4 with a kart my father built for me.
I competed in Formula 1 from 1984 until my death.
I won three Formula 1 World Championships with McLaren.
I died in a crash at the San Marino Grand Prix at Imola on May 1, 1994.

name: Michael Schumacher
clues: I was born in 1969 in Hürth, West Germany.
I started karting as a child at a track run by my father.
I am a retired German Formula 1 driver.
I won a record seven Formula 1 World Championships.
I suffered a severe brain injury in a skiing accident in the French Alps in December 2013.

name: Mike Tyson
clues: I was born in 1966 in Brooklyn, New York, USA.
I was discovered by trainer Cus D'Amato after being arrested over 30 times as a teenager.
I am a retired American heavyweight boxer.
At 20 years old, I became the youngest heavyweight champion in history.
I am known as 'Iron Mike' and famously bit off a piece of Evander Holyfield's ear during a fight.

name: Kobe Bryant
clues: I was born in 1978 in Philadelphia, USA, and spent part of my childhood in Italy.
My father was also a professional basketball player.
I was drafted directly out of high school into the NBA in 1996.
I won 5 NBA championships, all with the Los Angeles Lakers.
I tragically died in a helicopter crash in January 2020, along with my daughter Gianna.

name: Michael Jackson
clues: I was born in 1958 in Gary, Indiana, USA.
I began performing with my brothers as a child before going solo.
I am an American singer, songwriter, and dancer.
My album 'Thriller' (1982) remains the best-selling album of all time.
I am known as the King of Pop and popularized the 'moonwalk' dance move.

name: Elvis Presley
clues: I was born in 1935 in Tupelo, Mississippi, USA, into a working-class family.
My family moved to Memphis, Tennessee when I was 13.
I became one of the most significant cultural icons of the 20th century.
My 1956 hit 'Heartbreak Hotel' was my first number-one single.
I am known as 'The King of Rock and Roll' and my home, Graceland, is a famous landmark.

name: Freddie Mercury
clues: I was born in 1946 in Stone Town, Zanzibar, to Parsi Indian parents.
I grew up partly in India and later moved to England.
I was the lead vocalist of the rock band Queen.
I wrote 'Bohemian Rhapsody', one of the greatest rock songs ever recorded.
I died of AIDS-related complications in November 1991, aged 45.

name: Bob Marley
clues: I was born in 1945 in Nine Mile, Saint Ann Parish, Jamaica.
My father was a white Jamaican plantation overseer and my mother was Black Jamaican.
I am a musician and cultural icon who popularized reggae music worldwide.
My album 'Exodus' (1977) was named Album of the Century by Time magazine.
My most famous songs include 'No Woman, No Cry', 'One Love', and 'Redemption Song'.

name: David Bowie
clues: I was born in 1947 in Brixton, London, England.
I have a distinctive appearance including two different-coloured eyes, the result of a childhood fight.
I was a British singer, songwriter and actor known for constantly reinventing my image.
My alter ego 'Ziggy Stardust' became one of the most iconic personas in rock history.
My final album 'Blackstar' was released just two days before my death in January 2016.

name: Eminem
clues: I was born in 1972 in St. Joseph, Missouri, USA.
I grew up in poverty in Detroit, Michigan, and dropped out of high school.
I am a white rapper who broke into a genre historically dominated by Black artists.
My 2002 film '8 Mile' was loosely based on my own life.
I am the best-selling rapper of all time and known for my alter ego 'Slim Shady'.

name: Beyoncé
clues: I was born in 1981 in Houston, Texas, USA.
My father managed my early career and pushed me from a very young age.
I first rose to fame as the lead singer of the girl group Destiny's Child.
I have won more Grammy Awards than any other artist in history.
I am married to rapper Jay-Z and together we are one of music's most powerful couples.

name: Madonna
clues: I was born in 1958 in Bay City, Michigan, USA.
I moved to New York City in 1978 with just $35 in my pocket.
I am an American singer, songwriter, and actress.
I have sold over 300 million records worldwide, making me the best-selling female music artist of all time.
I am known as the 'Queen of Pop' and my 1984 MTV VMAs performance of 'Like a Virgin' was iconic.

name: Rihanna
clues: I was born in 1988 in Saint Michael, Barbados.
I was discovered by an American music producer during his visit to Barbados.
I am a singer, actress, and businesswoman.
My song 'Umbrella' (2007) became one of the best-selling singles of all time.
I founded Fenty Beauty in 2017, a cosmetics brand valued at over $2.8 billion.

name: Taylor Swift
clues: I was born in 1989 in West Reading, Pennsylvania, USA.
I moved to Nashville at 14 to pursue a country music career.
I am a singer-songwriter who crossed over from country into pop.
My 'Eras Tour' (2023–2024) became the highest-grossing concert tour of all time.
I have won 14 Grammy Awards, including a record four Album of the Year awards.

name: Kurt Cobain
clues: I was born in 1967 in Aberdeen, Washington, USA.
I had a troubled childhood following my parents' divorce.
I was the lead singer and guitarist of a band that defined the grunge movement.
My band Nirvana's album 'Nevermind' (1991) propelled alternative rock into the mainstream.
I died on April 5, 1994, at age 27, and am a member of the so-called '27 Club'.

name: Adele
clues: I was born in 1988 in Tottenham, London, England.
I studied at the BRIT School for Performing Arts in London.
I am a British singer-songwriter known for my powerful voice and emotional ballads.
My album '21' spent 24 weeks at number one in the UK.
My song 'Hello' broke the record for most Spotify streams in its first week when released in 2015.

name: Leonardo DiCaprio
clues: I was born in 1974 in Los Angeles, California, USA.
My Italian father and German mother met at an art exhibit in Los Angeles.
I am an American actor and environmental activist.
I starred in 'Titanic' (1997), one of the highest-grossing films of all time.
I won my first Academy Award for Best Actor for 'The Revenant' in 2016, after multiple nominations.

name: Marilyn Monroe
clues: I was born in 1926 in Los Angeles, California, and spent much of my childhood in foster homes.
I was discovered as a model before transitioning to acting.
I am an American actress, model, and singer who became a cultural icon.
My rendition of 'Happy Birthday, Mr. President' for John F. Kennedy is one of history's most famous performances.
I died in 1962 at age 36, and the image of me in a white dress over a subway grate is iconic.

name: Charlie Chaplin
clues: I was born in 1889 in Southwark, London, England.
I grew up in severe poverty, often living in workhouses as a child.
I am a British actor and filmmaker who became a legend of the silent film era.
My character 'The Tramp' is one of the most recognized images in cinema history.
My film 'The Great Dictator' (1940) was a satirical portrayal of Adolf Hitler.

name: Tom Hanks
clues: I was born in 1956 in Concord, California, USA.
I had a difficult childhood, moving frequently after my parents' divorce.
I am an American actor known for my approachable, everyman roles.
I am one of only two actors to win back-to-back Academy Awards for Best Actor.
I starred in 'Forrest Gump' (1994) and 'Cast Away' (2000), both iconic films.

name: Morgan Freeman
clues: I was born in 1937 in Memphis, Tennessee, USA.
I declined a full scholarship to study drama and joined the US Air Force instead.
I am an American actor and narrator known for my distinctive deep voice.
I won an Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor for 'Million Dollar Baby' (2004).
I am one of Hollywood's most recognizable narrators and have voiced God in two major films.

name: Arnold Schwarzenegger
clues: I was born in 1947 in Thal, Austria.
I won the Mr. Universe title at age 20, the youngest ever to do so.
I am an Austrian-American actor, businessman, and politician.
I starred in the Terminator franchise, delivering the iconic line 'I'll be back.'
I served as the 38th Governor of California from 2003 to 2011.

name: Will Smith
clues: I was born in 1968 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, USA.
I began my career as a rapper before transitioning to acting.
I am an American actor who starred in blockbuster franchises including Men in Black and Bad Boys.
I won an Academy Award for Best Actor for 'King Richard' (2022).
At the 2022 Oscars, I slapped presenter Chris Rock on stage in front of a global audience.

name: Albert Einstein
clues: I was born in 1879 in Ulm, in the Kingdom of Württemberg, Germany.
Contrary to popular belief, I was not a poor student — I clashed with rigid teaching methods.
I am a theoretical physicist who fundamentally changed our understanding of the universe.
I developed the theory of general relativity and the famous equation E=mc².
I won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 and am the most recognizable scientist in history.

name: Nelson Mandela
clues: I was born in 1918 in Mvezo, a small village in South Africa.
I trained as a lawyer and became involved in anti-apartheid activism.
I am a South African political leader and statesman.
I spent 27 years in prison — largely on Robben Island — for my fight against apartheid.
I became South Africa's first democratically elected president in 1994 and won the Nobel Peace Prize.

name: Martin Luther King Jr.
clues: I was born in 1929 in Atlanta, Georgia, USA.
I entered Morehouse College at just 15 years old.
I am an American minister and civil rights leader.
I led the Montgomery Bus Boycott in 1955 and organized the March on Washington in 1963.
I delivered the 'I Have a Dream' speech and was assassinated in Memphis in April 1968.

name: Mahatma Gandhi
clues: I was born in 1869 in Porbandar, in what is now the state of Gujarat, India.
I studied law in London and worked as a lawyer in South Africa for 21 years.
I am an Indian lawyer, anti-colonial nationalist, and political ethicist.
I led India's independence movement against British rule using non-violent civil disobedience.
I was assassinated on January 30, 1948, just months after India gained independence.

name: Marie Curie
clues: I was born in 1867 in Warsaw, Poland, then part of the Russian Empire.
I moved to Paris in 1891 to study science, as women could not attend university in Poland.
I am a physicist and chemist who conducted pioneering research on radioactivity.
I am the first and only person to win Nobel Prizes in two different sciences: Physics and Chemistry.
I discovered the elements polonium and radium, naming polonium after my homeland.

name: Nikola Tesla
clues: I was born in 1856 in Smiljan, in what is now Croatia.
I moved to the United States in 1884 with just four cents in my pocket.
I am an inventor, electrical engineer, and futurist.
I developed the alternating current (AC) electrical system now used worldwide.
I had a famous rivalry with Thomas Edison and died alone in a New York hotel room in 1943.

name: Charles Darwin
clues: I was born in 1809 in Shrewsbury, England.
My father wanted me to become a doctor, but I dropped out of medical school.
I am an English naturalist and biologist.
My 5-year voyage on the HMS Beagle laid the foundation for my later scientific work.
I proposed the theory of evolution by natural selection in my 1859 book 'On the Origin of Species'.

name: Napoleon Bonaparte
clues: I was born in 1769 in Ajaccio, Corsica, just months after France acquired the island from Genoa.
I was mocked for my Corsican accent when I arrived at a French military school at age 9.
I am a French military commander and political leader.
I declared myself Emperor of the French in 1804 and conquered much of Europe.
I was finally defeated at the Battle of Waterloo in 1815 and exiled to Saint Helena, where I died in 1821.

name: Steve Jobs
clues: I was born in 1955 in San Francisco, California, and was adopted as an infant.
I dropped out of Reed College after one semester but kept attending a calligraphy class.
I am an American business magnate and co-founder of a technology company.
I co-founded Apple Inc. in a garage in 1976 and later returned to save it from bankruptcy.
I unveiled the first iPhone in 2007, revolutionizing the smartphone industry, and died of pancreatic cancer in 2011.

name: Bill Gates
clues: I was born in 1955 in Seattle, Washington, USA.
I enrolled at Harvard University but dropped out after two years.
I am an American business magnate and philanthropist.
I co-founded Microsoft with Paul Allen in 1975.
My charitable foundation is one of the largest private philanthropic organizations in the world.

name: Elon Musk
clues: I was born in 1971 in Pretoria, South Africa.
I taught myself to code at age 10 and sold my first video game, Blastar, at age 12.
I am a business magnate and investor.
I founded SpaceX in 2002 and co-founded Tesla Motors in 2003.
I purchased Twitter (now X) in 2022 for $44 billion and became the wealthiest person in the world.

name: Mark Zuckerberg
clues: I was born in 1984 in White Plains, New York, USA.
My father, a dentist, hired a software developer to tutor me as a child.
I am an American technology entrepreneur.
I launched a social networking website from my Harvard dorm room in February 2004.
I co-founded Facebook (now Meta) and became one of the youngest self-made billionaires in history.

name: Barack Obama
clues: I was born in 1961 in Honolulu, Hawaii, USA.
My father was from Kenya and my mother was from Kansas.
I am an American politician and attorney.
I was elected as a U.S. Senator from Illinois in 2004.
I became the 44th President of the United States in 2009, the first African American to hold the office.

name: Winston Churchill
clues: I was born in 1874 at Blenheim Palace, Oxfordshire, England.
I was considered a poor student but excelled at English and showed military aptitude.
I am a British statesman, soldier, and writer.
I served as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom during World War II.
I am famous for my wartime speeches and won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1953.

name: Vladimir Putin
clues: I was born in 1952 in Leningrad, Soviet Union, now Saint Petersburg, Russia.
I practised judo and sambo from a young age and hold a black belt.
I am a Russian politician and former intelligence officer.
I served as a KGB foreign intelligence officer for 16 years before entering politics.
I have dominated Russian politics since 2000, serving as both President and Prime Minister.

name: Leonardo da Vinci
clues: I was born in 1452 in Vinci, a small town in the Republic of Florence.
I was the illegitimate son of a notary and a peasant woman.
I am an Italian polymath of the Renaissance, excelling in art, science, and engineering.
I painted the Mona Lisa and The Last Supper, two of the most famous artworks in history.
My notebooks contain sketches of flying machines, tanks, and solar power centuries before their invention.

name: Stephen Hawking
clues: I was born in 1942 in Oxford, England, on the 300th anniversary of Galileo's death.
I was diagnosed with motor neurone disease at age 21 and given just two years to live.
I am a British theoretical physicist and cosmologist.
I held the position of Lucasian Professor of Mathematics at Cambridge, the same chair once held by Newton.
My book 'A Brief History of Time' (1988) sold over 25 million copies worldwide.

name: Oprah Winfrey
clues: I was born in 1954 in rural Mississippi, USA, in extreme poverty.
I was the first Black female TV anchor in Nashville, Tennessee.
I am an American talk show host, actress, and businesswoman.
My talk show ran for 25 seasons from 1986 to 2011 and was watched by 48 million viewers weekly.
I became the first Black woman billionaire in North America.
